choices.title: You attempt to speak to the loot hoarder.

choice.title: Har-Har?
choice.response: Har-Har Djinx.

choice.title: ( Scratch your head. )
choice.response: Haha, you clearly don't speak Orcish.

choice.title: ( Sigh and roll your eyes. )
choice.response: Haha, you clearly don't speak Orcish.

choice.title: Die you evil green thing!
choice.response: Why you motherwrecker!

choice.title: What, you can understand me?
choice.response: Of course. I may be short but I'm not stupid.

choice.title: So, uhm, is your name Har-Har?
choice.response: No you simpleton. You said hello...

choice.title: And?
choice.response: I said hello hello back to you.

choice.title: Ohh, what about that Djinx thing?
choice.response: Long legs.

choice.title: Ahh, hello hello long legs.
choice.response: Wow, perhaps you're one step above simpleton.

choice.title: Uhh, are all greenskins as rude as you?
I have long legs and you call me long legs...
choice.response: You can't call me a greenskin!

choice.title: Ok, how about shorty then?
choice.response: No. That's not ok.

choice.title: Wait, why can you call me long legs?
choice.response: Because you have long legs.

choice.title: Why can't I call you shorty?
choice.response: Because I have short legs.

choice.title: And?
choice.response: That's condescending coming from you.

choice.title: So, what can I call you?
choice.response: You can call me long ears.

choice.title: Ok, how do I say hello to you in Orcish?
choice.response: Har-Har Zer Djigr.

choice.title: So, Zer Djigr means long ears then?
choice.response: No you silly. Djigr means long ears.

choice.title: Then what does Zer mean?
choice.response: Zer is my preferred pronoun.

choice.title: Uhh, I'm glad we cleared that out...
choice.response: All hail Orkhanada!

choice.title: Uhh, what?
choice.response: Are you deaf, all hail Orkhanada!

choice.title: So, Orkhanada, Is that a place or a person?
choice.response: It's the Orcish nation.

choice.title: A place, area or country then?
choice.response: Well, it's not a real country but...

choice.title: What then?
choice.response: Anywhere where there are orcs in power.

choice.title: Oh, that would be nowhere then.
choice.response: Very soon it will be everywhere.

choice.title: What do you mean with everywhere?
choice.response: Some time ago we were fighting dwarves.

choice.title: Hmm, dwarves, never heard of them.
choice.response: And you never will because they're all dead.

choice.title: So, you were fighting dwarves and then what?
choice.response: During the battle of Craghoe...

choice.title: Go on.
choice.response: Or the mountain with a wide canyon.

choice.title: Hah, Craghoe with a wide canyon.
choice.response: Stop interrupting me you simpleton.

choice.title: Uhuh, and then what?
choice.response: A young shaman turned all dwarves to stone.

choice.title: Ok, ok, what happened then?
choice.response: A young shaman turned all dwarves to stone.

choice.title: And this relates to Orkhanada how?
choice.response: Thousands of orcs were slaughtered every day...

choice.title: Go on.
choice.response: In the neverending battle against the dwarves.

choice.title: Ohh.
choice.response: And now we're building our armies...

choice.title: Go on.
choice.response: In dwarven strongholds with their equipment.

choice.title: Hmm, that sounds bad.
choice.response: Soon we will implement the green new deal...

choice.title: And what deal is that?
choice.response: If you're not green you die!

choice.title: Well, it's been nice talking to you.
choice.response: Wait, before you leave, take this.

choice.title: What's that?
choice.response: It's a dwarven rune stone.

choice.title: Oh, thank you, but there's no rune on it.
choice.response: It's been ground off. Take it to your shaman...

choice.title: And?
choice.response: And carve a new one while binding a soul to it.

choice.title: You can do that?
choice.response: Any shaman can do that.

choice.title: Well, thanks, but now I have to...
Take all your stuff for the rogue clan.
choice.response: Why you motherwrecker!
